title: Muscle Building - Cutting Program
description: A controlled caloric deficit designed to lose fat while preserving lean muscle mass. Perfect for getting lean and defined.
overview: Cutting is the process of reducing body fat while maintaining muscle through a caloric deficit combined with strength training. This program emphasizes protein intake to preserve muscle during the cut. Expected fat loss: 0.5-1 kg per week.

weekly_schedule.exercises: Bench Press (4x4), Incline Dumbbell Press (3x6), Cable Crossovers (3x12), Tricep Dips (3x8), Rope Extensions (3x12)
Deadlifts (4x3), Weighted Pull-ups (4x5), Barbell Rows (3x6), Preacher Curls (3x10), Machine Rows (3x10)
30min moderate intensity cardio + 15min core work (planks, leg raises, cable woodchops)
Squats (4x4), Romanian Deadlifts (3x6), Leg Press (3x8), Leg Curls (3x10), Calf Raises (4x12)
Military Press (4x4), Lateral Raises (3x12), Face Pulls (3x15), Barbell Curls (3x6), Machine Rows (3x10)
Light to moderate intensity cardio: running, cycling, rowing, or swimming

nutrition.content: 3 egg whites + 1 whole egg + 1 slice whole wheat toast + ½ cup oatmeal
200g lean chicken breast + 150g white rice + 200g green vegetables
40g whey protein + 40g white rice + minimal fat
200g white fish or lean beef + 150g sweet potato + 200g broccoli

tips: PRIORITIZE PROTEIN - eat it at every meal to preserve muscle
Track calories religiously - use a food scale
Strength training is essential - maintain strength to keep muscle
Cardio 3-4 times per week for fat loss without overdoing it
Stay hydrated and drink plenty of water to manage hunger
Get adequate sleep (7-9 hours) for recovery and hormone optimization
Be patient - rapid cuts lead to muscle loss
Take progress photos and measurements - scale can be misleading
If energy is too low, add 100-200 calories from carbs
Plan cheat meals strategically (1x per week maximum)
